# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

| Version | Supported |
| -------- | --------- |
| 1.x.x | ✅ |
| < 1.0 | ❌ |


# Reporting a Vulnerability

If you discover a security vulnerability in SIDRA, please do **NOT** create a public GitHub Issue.

Instead report it privately.

Email:

security@sidra.com

or

founder@sidra.com

Include:

- Vulnerability Description
- Steps to Reproduce
- Screenshots (if applicable)
- Impact
- Suggested Fix


# Scope

This policy covers:

- Website
- Founder Admin Panel
- Seller Dashboard
- Customer Dashboard
- Firebase Authentication
- Firestore
- Cloud Functions
- Firebase Storage
- APIs
- CMS
- Canvas Engine
- Payment Integration
- Notifications
- Analytics
- Search
- Security Rules


# Response Timeline

Initial Response

Within 48 Hours

Investigation

Within 7 Days

Critical Issues

Immediate Priority


# Disclosure

Please allow sufficient time for the vulnerability to be fixed before public disclosure.


# Out of Scope

The following are generally considered out of scope:

- Social Engineering
- Physical Attacks
- Spam Reports
- Denial of Service
- Missing Security Headers on localhost
- Third-party Service Outages


# Security Principles

SIDRA follows:

- Principle of Least Privilege
- Zero Trust
- Secure by Default
- Defense in Depth
- Encryption in Transit
- Encryption at Rest
- Role-Based Access Control
- Firestore Security Rules
- Firebase Authentication
- Continuous Dependency Monitoring
